data.title: Controversial AI 'Actor' Tilly Norwood Responds To Critics With New Song Saying 'AI's Not The Enemy, It's The Key'
data.description:
An artificial intelligence "actor" is back in the spotlight with a new music video fueling Hollywood's debate over AI performers.

London-based production company Particle6 recently released "Take The Lead" on YouTube, starring its digital persona Tilly Norwood.

The song pushes back on claims that AI performers lack creativity, emotion and a place in entertainment. It arrives as actors and labor groups continue criticizing AI's role in film.

Don't Miss:

A Music Video That Doubles As A Statement

The video shows Norwood in stylized scenes, including a paparazzi chase, a concert performance and a ride through the air on an inflatable pink flamingo. It opens with "When they talk about me, they don't see/ The human spark, the creativity," then "I'm just a tool, but I've got life."

The lyrics also include, "Don't be left out, don't fall behind," and "AI's not the enemy, it's the key."

A disclaimer at the start of the video says it was "made by 18 real humans," and Particle6 said the vocals were generated using the AI music tool Suno.

"Can’t wait to go to the Oscars!" The YouTube description reads, though Norwood is not part of any film or nominated project.

Trending: Skip the Regrets: The Essential Retirement Tips Experts Wish Everyone Knew Earlier.

Why The AI Fight Keeps Growing

Criticism extends beyond Norwood. "America's creative community is the envy of the world," the Human Artistry Campaign said in its Jan. 22 announcement launching the "Stealing Isn't Innovation" initiative against unauthorized AI use in creative work.

The group said some technology companies were using creators' works "without authorization or payment" to build generative AI platforms.

"Partnership and collaboration, including free market licensing of valuable works that the AI companies wish to use, isn't optional, but consistent with established law," the campaign said, arguing that licensing offers a path forward.

Actor Matthew McConaughey reportedly moved to protect his voice and likeness from AI misuse, part of a broader push by performers to control their identities.

Creator Says AI Still Needs Humans

"AI content isn’t instant — it always takes good ideas, taste, direction, judgement and time. In other words: people remain at the heart of it," she said.

For readers looking to explore how these innovation trends are influencing markets, thematic ETFs like those from Direxion provide a way to track companies driving automation, AI, and next-generation technologies—letting everyday investors participate in the sectors powering this new era.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: $100 Invested In Shopify 10 Years Ago Would Be Worth This Much Today
data.description:
Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 33.09% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 45.44%. Currently, Shopify has a market capitalization of $152.87 billion.

Buying $100 In SHOP: If an investor had bought $100 of SHOP stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $4,292.13 today based on a price of $117.22 for SHOP at the time of writing.

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.
